name: ECDLP verified knowledge graph
purpose: A machine-readable, navigable index of the machine-checked (Lean 4 + Mathlib, no sorry, no axioms) structure of the ECDLP landscape for secp256k1: proved theorems, their import dependencies, the corpus claims they verify, and the formalization barriers that bound them. Built for a future automated reasoner to load the known-and-verified frontier without re-deriving it.
invariant: Every theorem listed is accepted by the Lean kernel with no sorry and no added axioms. Truth is decided only by the kernel; this graph is derived from the ledger, it does not assert proofs.

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Proved.cube_root_of_eigenvalue
theorems.claim: GLV eigenvalue is a cube root of unity (ring form)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.collisionSet_card_le_one
theorems.claim: generic-group: distinct affine forms collide ≤ once

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.generic_dlog_sqrt_bound
theorems.claim: generic-group lower bound, square-root form `√p ≤ q

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.generic_success_le
theorems.claim: quantitative Shoup bound: success count ≤ q·q−q+1

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.pollard_rho_collision
theorems.claim: Pollard rho: a collision exists within `card` steps (pigeonhole)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.pollard_rho_periodic
theorems.claim: Pollard rho ρ-shape: sequence is eventually periodic

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.eval_add
theorems.claim: model soundness: `eval` is additive on forms (group mult ↔ form add)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.eval_neg
theorems.claim: model soundness: `eval` respects negation (group inverse ↔ form neg)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.eval_zero
theorems.claim: model soundness: identity is the zero form

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Secp256k1Schnorr.secp256k1_schnorr_extract
theorems.claim: Schnorr soundness over secp256k1 scalar field

theorems.name: Ecdlp.DLEQ.chaum_pedersen_verify
theorems.claim: Chaum–Pedersen DLEQ (equality of discrete logs) — completeness

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1.IsElliptic` (instance)
theorems.claim: secp256k1 is a Mathlib `EllipticCurve` (grounds the group law)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.adaptor_extract
theorems.claim: Adaptor signature witness extraction (atomic swaps / Lightning)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Proved.orderOf_eigenvalue_eq_three
theorems.claim: GLV eigenvalue has order exactly 3 (primitive cube root)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_beta_orderOf
theorems.claim: secp256k1 `β` has order 3 in `𝔽_p` (GLV CM generator)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_lambda_orderOf
theorems.claim: secp256k1 `λ` has order 3 in `ℤ/n` (GLV CM generator)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_three_cube_roots_of_unity
theorems.claim: X³ = 1` has exactly 3 roots in `𝔽_p` (GLV cube-root count)

theorems.name: Secp256k1.generator_on_curve
theorems.claim: secp256k1 base point `G` is on the curve (`Gy ≡ Gx³+7 mod p`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_generator_equation
theorems.claim: secp256k1 generator is a point of the Mathlib `EllipticCurve

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_generator_nonsingular
theorems.claim: secp256k1 generator is nonsingular (a group element)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Primality.secp256k1_p_prime
theorems.claim: secp256k1 field prime `p` is prime (full Pratt certificate)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Primality.secp256k1_n_prime
theorems.claim: secp256k1 group order `n` is prime (full Pratt certificate)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.PohligHellman.component
theorems.claim: Pohlig–Hellman: component depends only on `x mod d

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_embedding_degree_gt_100
theorems.claim: secp256k1 has no small embedding degree (`p^k ≢ 1 mod n` for `1≤k≤100`; MOV/FR resistance)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_trace_ordinary_nonanomalous
theorems.claim: secp256k1 trace of Frobenius: ordinary, non-anomalous, Hasse (`t≠0`, `t≠1`, `t≤4p`; Smart/SSSA + supersingular resistance)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Ψ₃
theorems.claim: secp256k1 3-division polynomial `Ψ₃ = 3X⁴+84X` (3-torsion `E[3]`; the CM-by-ℤ[ζ₃] / GLV structure)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.GenericGroup.collision_modEq
theorems.claim: collision equation `a+xb ≡ c+xd (mod n)` (rho/BSGS solve step)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Ψ₂Sq_ne_zero
theorems.claim: Ψ₂Sq ≠ 0` (2-torsion is a proper finite set)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Ψ₃_natDegree
theorems.claim: deg Ψ₃ = 4` (3-torsion count `#E[3] ≤ 9`; GLV-relevant CM torsion)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.pedersen_vector_homomorphic
theorems.claim: vector Pedersen commitment homomorphism (Bulletproofs / confidential tx)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.adaptor_complete
theorems.claim: adaptor signature completeness (atomic swaps / Lightning PTLC)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.mem_torsionBy_iff_addOrderOf_dvd
theorems.claim: E[n]` = points of order dividing `n` (`P∈E[n] ⟺ ord P ∣ n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.zmod_module_nsmul_eq_zero
theorems.claim: a `ZMod n`-module is killed by `n` (`n • x = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.torsionBy_eq_top
theorems.claim: the DL group is its own `n`-torsion (`G[n] = ⊤`; cofactor-1 shape)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.torsionBy_eq_ker_nsmul
theorems.claim: E[n] = ker[n]` (torsion = kernel of the multiplication-by-`n` endomorphism)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.zmultiples_le_torsionBy
theorems.claim: ⟨G⟩ ⊆ E[n]` (base-point subgroup lies in the `n`-torsion when `ord G ∣ n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_torsionBy_eq_ker_nsmul
theorems.claim: secp256k1 `E[n] = ker[n]` (torsion = kernel of `[n]` on the curve group)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_G_ne_zero
theorems.claim: secp256k1 base point `G ≠ O` (SEC2 generator as a non-zero curve point)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Ψ₃_ne_zero
theorems.claim: Ψ₃ ≠ 0` (3-torsion is a proper finite set)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_torsionBy_dvd_le
theorems.claim: secp256k1 torsion filtration (`E[m] ≤ E[n]` when `m ∣ n`, curve-named)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_preΨ₄_natDegree
theorems.claim: deg preΨ₄ = 6` (4-torsion bound up the tower)

barriers.title: No oracle / group-operation cost model in Lean
barriers.missing_foundation: a general cost / oracle-query model in Mathlib
barriers.blocks: exact Theta running times, index-calculus subexponential bounds, distinguished-point parallel speedups
barriers.partial_progress: generic-group Omega(sqrt p) lower bound and O(sqrt n) upper bounds are formalized: their information-theoretic core sidesteps a general cost model (collision count over affine forms a + b*X).

barriers.title: No quantum-circuit cost model in Mathlib
Summation / Semaev polynomials not in Mathlib
barriers.missing_foundation: elliptic summation polynomials S_n over MvPolynomial
barriers.blocks: index calculus on elliptic curves over extension fields
barriers.partial_progress: Mathlib has multivariate polynomials but not the S_n.

barriers.title: Weil/Tate pairing and isogeny depth missing
barriers.missing_foundation: Weil pairing on EllipticCurve, isogeny machinery
barriers.blocks: MOV/FR transfer reductions to finite-field DLP
barriers.partial_progress: Mathlib has the curve and isogeny base, not the pairing.

barriers.title: Concrete point count #E(F_p) = n not kernel-computable
barriers.missing_foundation: Schoof / efficient point counting in Mathlib
barriers.blocks: deriving #E = n abstractly for the concrete curve
barriers.partial_progress: the concrete order is instead pinned via native_decide / the published value; primality of n is machine-checked (Pratt).

corpus.note: 486 atomic claims. formalizable + formalizable_hard are the candidate frontier; informal_only / scope_meta are out of scope by nature.
